mapping.a_element: superharmonic perturbation of a Gaussian measure via logarithmic potential U^μ(z)
mapping.b_element: potential defining the eigenvalue distribution of random normal matrices

mapping.a_element: equilibrium measure supported on subharmonic generalized quadrature domains
mapping.b_element: asymptotic density of eigenvalues supported on the interior domain of a simple smooth curve

mapping.a_element: orthogonal polynomials with respect to weighted measure w(z) = exp(-|z|^2 + U^μ(z))
mapping.b_element: random normal matrix ensemble with spectrum constrained by the potential

mapping.a_element: Schwarz function of the curve bounding the support of the equilibrium measure
mapping.b_element: conformal mapping describing the boundary of the eigenvalue support

mapping.a_element: 2×2 matrix d-bar problem for orthogonal polynomials
mapping.b_element: rigorous analysis of eigenvalue density via complex analytic methods

brief_justification: Both papers study equilibrium measures arising from perturbations of a Gaussian measure (or equivalent potential) in the complex plane, with the support of the measure determined by a smooth curve. The machinery involves logarithmic potentials, conformal mappings, and the asymptotic distribution of zeros/eigenvalues governed by the same underlying variational principle in potential theory.
